site.name: Govardhan Reddy Narala
site.headline: I specialize in APIs, data pipelines, and scalable systems with measurable performance impact.
site.heroIntro: 3 years of experience building event-driven backend systems and distributed platforms processing 50M+ monthly transactions at 99.9% SLA.
heroSummary: I specialize in APIs, data pipelines, and scalable systems with measurable performance impact.

Most of my recent work has been in backend services, distributed integrations, full-stack applications, and data workflows that had to perform under real production constraints. This site is where I walk through what I built, the tradeoffs behind it, and the outcomes that mattered.
heroProofPoints: Built systems processing 50M+ monthly records and 10M+ monthly transactions at 99.9% SLA.
Reduced SQL query latency by 45% across data-intensive services and lowered MTTR to under 45 minutes.
Shipped full-stack and distributed systems using .NET, Node.js, Next.js, Kafka-style event flows, GraphQL, Redis, and Docker.
Combined backend engineering depth with analytics, ML research, observability, and automation.

homeValueCards.title: Product To Platform
homeValueCards.copy: I am comfortable working from user-facing flows down to service boundaries, data contracts, infrastructure, and the operational behavior behind them.

homeValueCards.title: Reliability Under Load
homeValueCards.copy: My background is grounded in production systems, so I design for uptime, observability, throughput, operational clarity, and failure handling from the start.

homeValueCards.title: Engineering Plus ML
homeValueCards.copy: I do not treat ML as a disconnected research artifact. I like connecting modeling, data pipelines, backend systems, and automation into end-to-end delivery.

aboutSections.title: How I Describe What I Do
aboutSections.copy: I see myself as a software engineer with strong backend, data, and ML-systems depth. I like building APIs, data pipelines, distributed workflows, and the reliability layer that makes those systems usable in production.

aboutSections.title: Why I Built This Portfolio This Way
aboutSections.copy: I did not want this portfolio to feel like a gallery of disconnected demos. I wanted it to show the actual shape of my work: enterprise backend systems, full-stack platform work, event-driven integrations, observability, and research-backed ML projects with real engineering context.

aboutSections.title: Where I Fit Best
aboutSections.copy: I am at my best in software engineering, full-stack, backend, data engineering, and ML-systems roles where system quality, measurable outcomes, and end-to-end ownership actually matter.

currentStatus.title: Open To Work
currentStatus.copy: I am actively exploring software engineering, full-stack, backend, data engineering, and ML-systems opportunities.

currentStatus.title: What I Want To Build
currentStatus.copy: I want to keep building production-grade platforms, backend services, and data-ML systems that balance reliability, scalability, and practical user value.

currentStatus.title: Working Style
currentStatus.copy: I do my best work in environments where I can bridge engineering and analytics, work through ambiguity, and turn complex system or data problems into clear delivery.

profile.headline: Software Engineer | Full Stack & Data/ML Systems | Python | SQL | C# .NET | Azure | Spark | PyTorch | 50M+ Transactions | 99.9% SLA
about: I am a software engineer with 3 years of experience building cloud-native systems, distributed integrations, data pipelines, and scalable applications. My strongest work sits where messy operational data, backend services, and production reliability meet, so I focus on building systems that are not just technically correct but actually usable in mission-critical workflows.

Across TCS and Hexagon, I worked with Azure, .NET, SQL, Node.js, Docker, Splunk, Snowflake, Kafka-style event flows, and integration platforms to ship systems handling 50M+ monthly records, 10M+ monthly transactions, 99.9% SLA targets, 400% traffic spikes, and sub-45-minute incident response.

My recent work also includes adversarial medical-imaging research accepted for publication and oral presentation at ICICT 2026, graph learning for disease clustering, a microservices-based real-time commerce platform using Node.js, GraphQL, Redis, FastAPI, and .NET, and an LLM-powered LinkedIn outreach agent using OpenAI and Ollama. I am currently exploring Software Engineer, Full Stack, Backend, Data Engineering, and ML-driven systems roles where I can build end-to-end platforms from backend services and data pipelines to automation and intelligent features.

highlights: Mentored 60+ students per semester in Python, SQL, databases, and big-data workflows through hands-on labs and office hours.
Simplified complex topics like query optimization, data pipelines, and debugging into practical problem-solving approaches that improved student performance and code quality.
Reviewed and graded 1000+ assignments, providing structured feedback that improved understanding and reduced recurring logical errors.

Architected 20+ enterprise integration workflows across Dell Boomi and MuleSoft, connecting Salesforce, NetSuite, Dynamics 365, Oracle EBS, and ServiceNow across CRM, ERP, ITSM, and e-commerce domains for 3 business units.

Engineered real-time event-driven pipelines using Kafka, JMS, AMQP, AS2, and EDI, processing 10M+ monthly transactions across payments, order management, shipment tracking, and fulfillment while maintaining 99.9% SLA uptime.

Built a unified log aggregation dashboard in C# and Python consolidating telemetry from Boomi, MuleSoft, and Infor ION, reducing cross-platform investigation time by 55% and lowering MTTR from 2 hours to under 45 minutes.

Automated Atom health monitoring using REST APIs, Bash, and Python with HTML alerting for crashed Atoms and stalled flows, cutting incident detection from hours to under 5 minutes and eliminating recurring outage patterns.
Implemented schema validation, transformation rules, and data consistency controls across ingestion pipelines feeding Snowflake and SQL Server, reducing downstream data failures by 47%.

Engineered a serverless passenger-processing pipeline using C#/.NET Core and Azure Durable Functions for 10+ global airline clients, reducing transaction resolution time by 38% at 99.9% SLA across 3 airports.
Built autoscaling event-driven architecture using Azure Event Hubs and Azure Data Factory processing 50M+ monthly records and absorbing 400% peak traffic spikes with zero manual intervention.
Developed real-time KQL and Power BI dashboards processing 2M+ daily passenger events, helping ops teams cut staffing overhead by 22% and reducing post-release incident rates by 45%.
Reduced query latency by 45% through indexing, schema refinement, and execution plan optimization across SQL Server, PostgreSQL, and Cosmos DB.
Built REST microservices using .NET Core and Dapper with clean architecture patterns, improving throughput and maintainability for distributed transaction workflows.

education.degree: Master of Science in Data Science
Bachelor of Technology in Mechanical Engineering

certifications: Programming for Everybody (Getting Started with Python)
Autodesk Certified Professional: AutoCAD for Design and Drafting Exam Prep

projects.title: Realtime Commerce Platform
projects.summary: Real-time event-driven commerce platform built as an 11-service ecosystem with GraphQL BFF, Redis, recommendations, and full cart-to-fulfillment lifecycle orchestration.
highlights: Architected an 11-service microservices ecosystem using Node.js, Next.js, .NET, FastAPI, GraphQL, and Redis around a real-time commerce domain.
Managed the full cart-to-fulfillment lifecycle with event-driven order processing, real-time inventory flows, customer storefront experience, and admin-side operational views.
Combined gateway, recommendations, search, caching, and asynchronous messaging patterns into a more production-like marketplace platform.

github.description: Docker-first real-time commerce platform with Node.js, Next.js, Redis, event streaming, GraphQL, .NET fulfillment, and recommendations.

projects.title: LinkedIn Outreach Agent
projects.summary: LLM-powered Chrome extension that reads live LinkedIn context and drafts profile-grounded outreach with browser automation and human review before send.
highlights: Built a Chrome extension that reads visible LinkedIn context and drafts recruiter replies, follow-ups, and connection outreach grounded in profile context.
Supported OpenAI, Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible local LLM servers so the tool can run in either cloud or privacy-first local mode.
Added browser automation for repetitive flows while keeping a review-first design so every message remains human-approved.

github.description: Natural LinkedIn replies, recruiter responses, and connection outreach with browser automation and local or cloud LLMs

projects.title: Adversarial Attacks on Deep Neural Networks
projects.summary: Medical imaging robustness benchmark evaluating adversarial attacks and defenses across four CNN architectures on the MURA X-ray dataset.
highlights: Benchmarked four CNN architectures on 40,561 MURA X-ray images across multiple perturbation levels using FGSM, PGD, and Carlini-Wagner attacks.
Built a preprocessing workflow with resizing, normalization, CLAHE enhancement, bone windowing, and smart cropping for clinically consistent inputs.
Implemented FGSM adversarial training with 50% clean and 50% adversarial batches over 50 epochs, improving FGSM robustness and yielding moderate PGD gains.

github.description: This project investigates both adversarial attacks and defensive strategies for Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). While adversarial examples exploit vulnerabilities in models by introducing imperceptible perturbations, defensive methods aim to improve robustness and ensure reliable predictions.

projects.title: Houston Crime Type Classification (2010-2024)
projects.summary: Large-scale crime analytics pipeline on roughly 3.6M Houston crime records to predict NIBRS offense categories under a realistic temporal holdout design.
highlights: Standardized and merged three differently formatted source datasets into one modeling-ready schema spanning 2010 to 2024.
Engineered Beat, Premise, Month, Weekday, and Hour Group features and cleaned multi-label crime records into separate observations.
Selected MLPClassifier as the best model, reaching 29.37% accuracy and 0.5895 macro AUC on the 2024 holdout set.

github.description: An end-to-end machine learning pipeline built to predict crime categories (NIBRS Description) using 14 years of Houston Police Department data (~3.6 million records), with 2024 used as a true future holdout year.

projects.title: Disease Networks for Disease Clustering
projects.summary: End-to-end graph learning pipeline that combines disease-gene networks with Gene Ontology features to improve unsupervised biomedical disease clustering.
highlights: Built disease x gene and disease x GO feature matrices to combine graph topology with biological process semantics.
Trained a 3-layer GCN encoder and clustered learned latent embeddings with KMeans for stronger disease-group separation.
Improved inter-vs-intra cluster distance separation from 0.90 to 1.45, 0.85 to 1.25, and 1.10 to 1.85 across 40, 50, and 60-disease subsets.

projects.title: CivicQuest
projects.summary: AI-supported community engagement platform for volunteering discovery, sustainability learning, and guided interaction.
highlights: AI-powered community engagement platform
Volunteer discovery and registration journeys
Gamified sustainability learning plus Azure bot integration

github.description: AI-powered community engagement platform that helps users discover local volunteering events, register seamlessly, learn sustainability through a gamified experience, and interact with an Azure-based chatbot — built with Node.js, Express, SQLite, and Azure services.

projects.title: Multimodal Book Genre Classification and Clustering
projects.summary: Multimodal content-intelligence pipeline that combines book titles and cover images to classify and segment books across large catalog datasets.
highlights: Built a dual-modality workflow using Word2Vec and LSTM for titles plus CNN-based visual features for covers across Book32 and BookCover30.
Performed large-scale preprocessing and missing-data analysis before classification and clustering.
Validated genre separation with KMeans and PCA, surfacing interpretable clusters such as Self-Help, Religion and Spirituality, and Education.

github.description: A scalable multimodal machine learning pipeline for book genre prediction, clustering, and segmentation using both book cover images and textual metadata (titles).  This project combines Computer Vision + NLP to automatically organize and segment large-scale book catalogs for recommendation systems and digital libraries.

projects.title: Zero Hunger Big Data Analytics Pipeline
projects.summary: Big-data pipeline aligned to UN SDG 2 that processes 86,657 records across hunger, agriculture, and socio-economic indicators into scalable analytics and forecasting workflows.
highlights: Designed ingestion, transformation, and querying workflows across HDFS, Hadoop Streaming MapReduce, Spark, and Hive.
Normalized public data sources including FAO, World Bank, and UNICEF-style datasets into analysis-ready tables for hunger hotspot detection.
Built forecasting and visualization workflows, with project materials reporting about 95% prediction accuracy for hunger-trend evaluation.

highlights: Maintained as a working reference repo
Useful for .NET architecture and API patterns

projects.summary: Enterprise integration and reliability work around Boomi-based distributed workflows, runtime observability, and support automation.
highlights: Supported 10M+ daily transactions across distributed runtimes while sustaining 99.9% SLA uptime for business-critical workflows.
Implemented validation and control checks across 6 ingestion pipelines to reduce downstream failures and improve data consistency.
Automated health checks, restart workflows, and runbooks to eliminate recurring failures and reduce onboarding time for new engineers.

github.description: For testing out the Boomi CI/CD
